care_notes: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil are dry. Likes bright, indirect light.
Very forgiving. Let soil dry out between waterings; droopy leaves mean it's thirsty.
Drought tolerant. Overwatering is the main killer; water sparingly in winter.
Stores water in rhizomes. Let soil dry completely between waterings.
Keep soil lightly moist. Brown tips often come from fluoride in tap water.
Dramatic wilter — it tells you when it's thirsty. Keep soil consistently moist.
Water thoroughly when top soil dries. Hates being moved and cold drafts.
Let the top few centimeters dry out. Wipe leaves to keep them glossy.
Allow topsoil to dry between waterings. Tolerates low light well.
Loves humidity and consistently moist soil. Never let it fully dry out.
Prefers distilled or rain water and high humidity. Keep soil lightly moist.
Succulent — water deeply but rarely, letting soil dry fully in between.
Water when soil is dry. Wrinkled leaves mean thirsty; soft leaves mean too wet.
Soak-and-dry method. Avoid getting water in the rosette.
Shrivelled pearls mean thirsty. Very prone to rot — err on the dry side.
Water when top soil dries. Slightly more water while budding and blooming.
Water when roots turn silvery. Never leave standing water in the pot.
Water from below with room-temperature water; wet leaves spot easily.
Keep soil lightly moist but not soggy. Good airflow prevents mildew.
Let soil mostly dry between waterings. Rotate regularly for even growth.
Water deeply, then let the top half of the soil dry out.
Drought tolerant. Sensitive to fluoride — filtered water avoids brown tips.
Let the top few centimeters dry out between waterings.
Very drought tolerant. Water sparingly, even less in winter.
Keep soil lightly moist in the growing season. Tolerates lower light.
Likes evenly moist soil and humidity. Don't let it sit in water.
Thirsty palm — keep soil consistently moist and give it bright light.
Water when the top few centimeters dry. Loves as much sun as you can give.
Keep soil evenly moist. Drops leaves when moved or left to dry out.
Water when topsoil dries. Sap is irritating — keep away from pets.
Easygoing. Let soil dry a bit between waterings; tolerates low light.
Chunky, well-draining soil. Water when the top feels dry; loves humidity.
Keep soil lightly moist and humidity high. May go dormant in winter.
Let topsoil dry slightly. Prefers cooler rooms; watch for spider mites.
Semi-succulent leaves. Let soil dry well between waterings.
Water when soil is mostly dry. Compact and happy in medium light.
Consistent watering; drops leaves in response to change or drafts.
Water when the pot feels light and topsoil is dry. Yellow leaves = too wet.
Succulent-like. Let soil dry between waterings; deadhead spent blooms.
Small succulent. Soak-and-dry; shade-tolerant compared to other succulents.
Water only when fully dry, barely at all in winter. Maximum sun.
No soil — soak for 20-30 minutes weekly, then dry upside down.
Thirsty herb. Keep soil moist and pinch flowers to keep leaves coming.
Likes consistently moist soil. Grows aggressively — keep it contained.
Mediterranean — prefers to dry out between waterings and lots of sun.
Hates wet feet. Sandy, fast-draining soil and full sun.
Keep soil lightly moist with soft water. Folds its leaves up at night.
Semi-succulent trailing vine. Let soil dry well between waterings.
Lightly moist soil. May die back and regrow from bulbs — that's normal.
Fast grower. Keep lightly moist; prune regularly to stay bushy.
